mnemonics.title: Von Neumann Parts
mnemonics.content: V NA C M B: Von Neumann Architecture = CPU, Memory (unified), Bus (single)

mnemonics.title: Bus Types
mnemonics.content: D A C: Data, Address, Control - THREE types of bus signals to remember

mnemonics.title: Interrupt Handling
mnemonics.content: I D R F: Interrupt Detection → Save State → Route to Handler → Finish and Return

acronyms.usage: Used for high-speed data transfer between I/O devices and memory without CPU involvement
Firmware that initializes hardware and starts the boot process
Critical interrupt that cannot be ignored, used for power failure and hardware errors
Multiple processors sharing memory and I/O with equal access to all resources
Multi-processor design where memory access time depends on memory location relative to processor
The interface between software and hardware defining available instructions
Hierarchical cache levels: L1 is fastest and smallest, L3 is slowest but largest

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a single-lane road with two-way traffic for both cars (data) and passengers (instructions) - this single path limits overall throughput
Think of a library: your desk is L1 (fastest, smallest), the shelves are L2, and the basement storage is main memory (slowest, largest)
Like a phone ringing during work: you pause (CPU saves state), answer (handle interrupt), and resume where you left off
Think of a delivery service: instead of you personally fetching packages (CPU involvement), a courier (DMA) delivers directly to your home (memory)
SMP is like multiple workers sharing one desk and filing cabinet; NUMA is like workers each having their own desk but sharing a common meeting room

analogies.analogy: Think of the system bus as a city's highway system - all traffic (data) moves between neighborhoods (components) using these roads
Like keeping frequently used kitchen items on the counter instead of in the pantry - faster access but limited space
Like a restaurant host who translates customer requests into kitchen instructions - mediates between requester and actual service
Like turning the key to start a car - it initiates all the internal systems before you're ready to drive
Like having a notification alert you when mail arrives rather than constantly checking the mailbox yourself
